task: Fix scripts/postinstall.mjs so Windows npm global installs correctly detect the npm prefix, use the right PATH separator, and persist the CodeMie bin path to the Windows user PATH (reusing src/utils/windows-path.ts), with exported helpers and new Vitest coverage.

key_reasoning.reason: Touches scripts/postinstall.mjs (export refactor of a previously side-effect-only script), vitest.config.ts, a new test file, and reuse/possible extension of src/utils/windows-path.ts — a shared utility already consumed by src/utils/native-installer.ts, which is what pushed this from M to L.

Acceptance criteria are unusually detailed (platform branch, separator, persistence mechanism, shim files, test file, no regression), but one real design decision is open: whether npm install -g should mimic install.ps1's isolated-prefix/wrapper-shim pattern or simply fix detection for npm's actual unmanaged global prefix — this needs resolving before implementation.

Two independent existing Windows PATH-persistence implementations already exist in the repo (install.ps1's .NET API, windows-path.ts's setx/reg.exe); reusing the already-tested, exported windows-path.ts keeps risk moderate, but no repo precedent exists for mocking raw child_process calls if that reuse path isn't taken.
Realistic footprint is small: postinstall.mjs (rewrite), vitest.config.ts (test.include glob fix), scripts/__tests__/postinstall.test.ts (new), and possibly a small addition to windows-path.ts.

red_flags_applied: Component Scope bumped from M (3) to L (4): touches core shared utility src/utils/windows-path.ts, which is consumed elsewhere by src/utils/native-installer.ts
